ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Flat bread) is the closest defensible category for roti prata; no specific code exists for hotdog prata as a composite dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for hotdog prata or roti prata as a specific food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of hotdog prata is approximately 180 g, equivalent to one piece of hotdog onion prata as commonly sold at Singapore hawker stalls and mamak restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Singapore Health Promotion Board Foodo Database - Hotdog Prata (cr_id G00010320)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) sourced directly from HPB Foodo database entry for Hotdog Prata (cr_id G00010320). Saturated fat, trans fat, and vitamin C values are from USDA FoodData Central for analogous ingredients (hotdog, margarine) at lower confidence; these are retained as best available estimates and flagged accordingly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on roti/flatbread made from refined wheat flour
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for hotdog prata. GI estimated at 70 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for roti/Indian flatbread made from refined wheat flour (GI ~70, high category). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI × available carbohydrate per serving / 100 = 70 × (75.23 × 1.80) / 100 = 70 × 135.41 / 100 ≈ 94.8. Serving size used: 180 g (default portion).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 632 mg/100 g exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold; processed hotdog sausage adds further sodium nitrates and saturated fat.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid hotdog prata due to its sodium content of 632 mg/100 g; if consumed, limit to a half portion and avoid high-sodium accompaniments such as curry dips.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate load (75.2 g/100 g) with a high estimated GI of 70 produces rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; trans fat (0.28 g/100 g) worsens insulin resistance.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Hotdog prata is unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes due to its high refined carbohydrate content, high glycemic index, and the presence of trans fat from margarine, all of which impair glycaemic control and insulin sensitivity.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 6.67 g/100 g and trans fat at 0.28 g/100 g both elevate LDL cholesterol; sodium at 632 mg/100 g compounds cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Hotdog prata should be avoided by individuals with cardiovascular disease due to its combination of high saturated fat, trans fat from margarine, and elevated sodium, all of which are established risk factors for atherosclerosis and hypertensive cardiovascular events.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Trans fat (0.28 g/100 g) is a hard disqualifier for rheumatoid arthritis; saturated fat at 6.67 g/100 g exceeds the 5 g/100 g pro-inflammatory threshold; processed hotdog sausage contains arachidonic acid precursors.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid hotdog prata entirely, as its trans fat content from margarine is strongly pro-inflammatory, and the processed hotdog sausage amplifies joint inflammation through sodium nitrates and arachidonic acid precursors.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Hotdog prata is a popular Singaporean hawker dish consisting of a flaky, layered flatbread (roti prata) that is pan-fried on a griddle with margarine or ghee and filled or topped with a sliced hotdog sausage. The prata dough is made from refined wheat flour, water, salt, and fat, stretched thin and folded repeatedly to create its characteristic flaky, crispy-edged layers. The hotdog sausage — typically a processed chicken or beef frankfurter — is placed inside or alongside the prata before or during cooking, imparting a savoury, slightly smoky flavour. The finished dish has a golden-brown, crispy exterior with a soft, chewy interior and a salty, meaty filling. It is commonly served with curry sauce or dhal for dipping. Hotdog prata is a fusion adaptation of the classic plain prata, reflecting Singapore's multicultural hawker culture where Indian-Muslim roti prata stalls have incorporated Western-style processed meats to appeal to a broader audience. Nutritionally, hotdog prata is energy-dense at 412 kcal/100 g, with a high refined carbohydrate load of 75.2 g/100 g, moderate protein at 9.85 g/100 g, and fat at 8.25 g/100 g including 6.67 g/100 g saturated fat and 0.28 g/100 g trans fat from margarine. Sodium is elevated at 632 mg/100 g. These characteristics make hotdog prata a high-concern food for individuals managing hypertension, type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. It is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans due to the processed meat content.

What is the sodium content of hotdog prata?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010320, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat, trans fat, and vitamin C are from USDA FoodData Central for analogous ingredients (hotdog, margarine) at lower confidence; these are flagged. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database based on refined wheat flour flatbread.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for hotdog prata is not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient data beyond those reported by HPB Foodo is not available for this specific dish in any approved source as of 2026-04-16. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as reported by HPB Foodo (confirmed zero per HPB source). Saturated fat (6.67 g/100 g), trans fat (0.28 g/100 g), and vitamin C (1.11 mg/100 g) are sourced from USDA FoodData Central for analogous ingredients (hotdog, margarine) at confidence 0.70; these values should be verified against a primary approved source when available. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on refined wheat flour flatbread from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for hotdog prata was found. Glycemic load per serving calculated using 180 g default portion: GL = 70 × (75.23 × 1.80) / 100 ≈ 94.8 (very high). Two portions are listed in the input (180 g and 200 g); 180 g is used as the default serving size. The input listed two entries with is_default: true — only 180 g is treated as default here.
